mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL indicates accelerating inflation, prompting expectations of tighter monetary policy that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of rising inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in UNRATE above 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in RSAFS and HOUST reflects robust consumer and housing demand that boosts corporate earnings forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60d returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO below its 12-month moving average indicates weakening industrial output that reduces near-term earnings momentum and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising FEDFUNDS and falling CPIAUCSL signals effective monetary tightening that anchors inflation expectations without stifling growth, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal predicts negative returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in PAYEMS reflects expanding employment that increases household income and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods with PAYEMS growth, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in RSAFS exceeding 2% signals a sudden contraction in retail sales that immediately dampens earnings revisions and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5d returns occur in >65% of triggered instances, or the threshold signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO alongside stable GDPC1 indicates improving industrial efficiency that lifts profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20d returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term is insignificant in predictive models.
